folio.label: 28r
folio.l: Hath preyed æ he mygút , ben of that game
And wel was hi~ , that ther to chosen was
For if there fel , to morwen swich a kaas
ñe knowen wele , that euäy lusty knygúxknygútknygúxt
That louetú ¹amours , and hath his mygút
Were it in Engelond , or elles where
They wolde here thankes , wyllen to be there
To figúten for a lady , benedicite
It were a lusty sigút , for to se
¢ And rigút so ferden they , with Palamon
With hi~ there went , knygútes many on
Some wold ben armed , in haberioun
And in a breestplate , and a ligút Iepoun
And some wold haue , a peyre plates large
And some wold haue , a spruce sheld & targe
And some wold ben armed , on hise legges wele
And haue an Ax , & some a mace of stele
There nys no newe gyse , æ it nas old
Armed were they , as I haue ©ow told
Euäych after , hise opyniou~
¢ There maist æè se , comyng w Palamou~
Lygurge hi~ self , the grete kyng of Trace
Blak was his berd , and manly was his face
The sercles of hise eyen , in his hede
They gloweden , betwix ©elwe & rede
And like a griffou~ , loked he a boute
With kempe heeres , on hise browes stoute
Hys lymes grete , hise braunes hard & strong
His shuldres brode , his armes grete & long
And as the gyse was , in his contre
Ful heye , vp on a chayer of gold , stod he
With foure white Boles , in the trays
In stede of Cotearmure , ouä his harneys
With nayles ©elwe , and brigút as eny gold
He had a beres skyn , cole blak for old
His long her , was kembed behynde his bak
As ony rauenes fether , it shone for blak
A wrethe of gold , arme gret , of huge weygút
Vp on his hede , and ful of stones brygút
Of fyne Rubies , and Diamauntes
A bouten his chaier , there went white alauntes
Twenty and mo , as grete as ony stere
To honten at the lyou~ , or the deere
And folwed hi~ , with mosellis faste I bounde
Colered of gold , and torettes fyled rounde
